Tags.HoverText: The reserved word output is a port definition providing a means of interconnecting hardware descriptions consisting of modules, primitives, and macromodules. For example, module X can instantiate module Y, using port connections appropriate to module X. These port names can differ from the names of the internal nets and registers specified in the definition of module Y.

Wire nets connect elements. The net-type wire is identical in syntax and function to the net-type tri. A wire net can be used for nets that are driven by a single gate or continuous assignment. Logical conflicts from multiple sources on a wire net result in unknown values unless the net is controlled by logic strength.
output wire [7:0] r
